basic_details.job_title: Goa PSC Tutor/Demonstrator Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 84 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Goa Public Service Commission (Goa PSC) Tutor/Demonstrator Recruitment 2026 Notification (Advt. No. 3A Year 2026) has been released on 20 March 2026 on the official website gpsc.goa.gov.in. A total of 84 vacancies have been announced through the Goa PSC Tutor/Demonstrator, Dietician and Insurance Medical Officer Recruitment 2026.

important_dates.3: Last Date for Submission of Application (All Posts)
important_dates.5: Last Date for SBI Challan Cash Payment at SBI Branch (Goa only)
important_dates.6: Last Date to Submit Stamped e-Challan to Commission's Office

application_fee.details: Application fee as applicable per GPSC norms. Online Payment Mode: Make online payment through the GPSC portal. Download e-receipt showing successful payment status. Offline Payment Mode (SBI Challan): Generate e-challan from the portal. Make cash payment at any SBI Branch situated in Goa only, on or before the last date (validity of e-challan is seven days from generation). Bank Stamp is essential on the e-challan. Stamped e-challan to be produced to the Commission's office on or before the last date of submission of application. Only after successful payment can candidates download their application form. Applications where fee payment has been made after the last date will be summarily rejected and no refund will be provided.

age_limit.details: Tutor/Demonstrator: Not exceeding 45 years. Dietician: Not exceeding 45 years. Insurance Medical Officer: Not exceeding 45 years. Age relaxation applicable for reserved category candidates as per Government of Goa rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Recognised Medical Qualification (MBBS) / Degree in Home Science / As per post-wise eligibility
qualification.details: Tutor/Demonstrator – Goa Medical College (Group A): Essential Qualifications: A recognised medical qualification included in the First or the Second Schedule or Part II of the Third Schedule (other than licentiate qualifications) to the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956 (Act No. 102 of 1956). Holders of qualifications included in Part II of the Third Schedule should also fulfil the conditions stipulated in sub-section (3) of Section 13 of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956. Knowledge of Konkani. Desirable Qualifications: Post-graduate degree or equivalent qualification in the speciality concerned from a National Medical Commission (then Medical Council of India) recognised medical college/institution. Knowledge of Marathi. Dietician – Directorate of Health Services: Essential Qualifications: Degree in Home Science of a recognised University or equivalent. Knowledge of Konkani. Desirable Qualifications: Diploma in Dietetics. Post-Graduate Diploma or Degree in Nutrition. Experience as Dietician in Hospital. Knowledge of Marathi. Insurance Medical Officer – ESI Scheme, Labour Department: Essential Qualifications: A recognised Medical qualification included in the First or the Second Schedule or Part II of the Third Schedule (other than Licentiate qualifications) to the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956 (Act 102 of 1956). Holders of qualifications included in Part II of the Third Schedule should fulfil the conditions stipulated in Section 13(3) of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956. Completion of compulsory rotating internship. Knowledge of Konkani. Desirable Qualifications: Experience in general medical practice. Knowledge of Marathi.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Tutor/Demonstrator in Casualty and Trauma Medicine
Insurance Medical Officer in ESI Scheme

salary_details.pay_scale: As per pay Matrix Level-10, As per pay matrix level 6, Rs. 15,600-39,100+5,400/- (pre-revised) (As per pay matrix level 10)
salary_details.details: Tutor/Demonstrator (Goa Medical College): As per Pay Matrix Level-10. Dietician (Directorate of Health Services): As per Pay Matrix Level-6. Insurance Medical Officer (ESI Scheme, Labour Department): Rs. 15,600–39,100 + Grade Pay Rs. 5,400/- (pre-revised); As per Pay Matrix Level-10.

how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply Online through the Goa Public Service Commission's official website: https://gpsc.goa.gov.in OR the Government of Goa portal: https://cbes.goa.gov.in/
Candidates must carefully read all "Instructions" available on the Commission's website before filling out the online application form.
After filling the application form appropriately, candidates must pay the application fee either Online or via Offline SBI Challan mode.
Only after successful fee payment can candidates download their application form.

Candidates choosing Offline (SBI Challan) payment must make cash payment at any SBI Branch in Goa only, before the last date, and submit the Bank-stamped e-challan to the Commission's office on or before 15 May 2026.
The last date for submission of applications for all posts is 15 May 2026.
Candidates must provide a valid email address and mobile number in the application form to receive all Commission communications.
important_instructions: Candidates should carefully read the "Instructions" available on the Commission's website https://gpsc.goa.gov.in before filling the application online. In case of non-availability of suitable candidates with the knowledge of Konkani, the Goa Public Service Commission may recommend a candidate if otherwise found fit and this requirement can be relaxed by the Government, on the recommendation of GPSC, in the case of Group 'A' posts in Professional Colleges and Consultants in the Directorate of Health Services. Prescribed Essential Qualifications (EQs) are minimum and mere possession of the same does not entitle candidates to be called for interview. Where the number of applications received is large, the Commission will short-list the candidates to be called for interview as mentioned in the "Instructions". Email address and mobile number of the candidate should invariably be provided in the application form. The Commission will send all communications concerning the candidate's application through the email address/mobile number provided. All candidates are advised to check their email accounts/mobile phones/Commission's website regularly for updates/notices concerning their application. If any discrepancy is observed in the translated version of the advertisement, the English version will be considered as authentic. In case of non-availability of candidates in the category of PwD (for Tutor/Demonstrator posts), an equal number of posts shall be kept vacant from the Unreserved category.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Goa PSC Tutor/Demonstrator recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the Goa PSC Tutor/Demonstrator posts is 15 May 2026. Ensure you apply well before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for Tutor/Demonstrator posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 84 vacancies announced by the Goa Public Service Commission for various Tutor/Demonstrator positions and other allied roles.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum qualification required for these posts?
faq.answer: The minimum qualification generally includes a recognised medical qualification as per the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956, and knowledge of Konkani. Specific details may vary for certain posts like Dietician.

faq.question: Q4. What is the maximum age limit for applicants?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all the posts advertised is 45 years. Age relaxation may be applicable as per government regulations.

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for the Goa PSC recruitment?
faq.answer: Interested candidates can apply online through the official Goa PSC website. The application process involves filling out the form, uploading documents, and submitting it before the last date.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification does not mention any specific application fee for this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to check the detailed notification for confirmation.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Tutor/Demonstrator posts?
faq.answer: The selection process typically involves shortlisting of candidates based on their applications, followed by an interview.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary offered for the Tutor/Demonstrator posts?
faq.answer: The salary for Tutor/Demonstrator posts will be as per Pay Matrix Level-10. Other posts like Dietician and Insurance Medical Officer have different pay scales.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be accessed through the 'Important Links' section of this article, where a direct link is provided.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website of Goa Public Service Commission?
faq.answer: The official website of the Goa Public Service Commission is http://gpsc.goa.gov.in. You can visit this site for more information and to apply.
